success_surface: OpenPyXL style copying and cell manipulation with multiple error recovery iterations
effective_behaviors: Systematically recover from openpyxl API errors through iterative code refinement
Use defensive attribute access (hasattr/getattr) before reading style properties
Construct new style objects with correct parameter types rather than direct assignment
Wrap style manipulation code in try/except blocks to prevent crashes
Verify critical cell values before writing final output
Dynamically locate target columns via header search rather than hardcoded positions
Clear target range before populating to avoid data contamination
Separate value copying from style copying logic for better debugging
evidence: Agent recovered from TypeError regarding StyleProxy by switching to explicit Font/PatternFill construction
Used getattr(source_cell.fill, 'fill_type', None) to safely detect fill patterns
Wrapped each style property copy operation in try/except blocks
Verified B6:B10 values after modification before saving file
Found '0-15' column at position 9 through header iteration loop
Cleared target column B with target_cell.value = None before populating
Built new Font/PatternFill/Alignment objects with explicit parameters

memory_items.title: Defensive Style Attribute Access
memory_items.lesson: Always use hasattr() or getattr() when accessing openpyxl style properties before reading their values
memory_items.generalization: openpyxl style objects have inconsistent attribute availability; guard all style property reads with defensive checks to prevent AttributeError exceptions during cell formatting operations.

memory_items.title: Style Object Construction Best Practices
memory_items.lesson: Create new style objects with correct parameter types rather than assigning raw style references
memory_items.generalization: When copying styles between cells in openpyxl, instantiate fresh Font/PatternFill/Border/Alignment objects with explicit parameters instead of directly assigning source cell style attributes, as this avoids type mismatch errors.

memory_items.title: Try/Except Protection for Format Operations
memory_items.lesson: Enclose all style/cell format modifications in try/except blocks to ensure partial failures don't crash execution
memory_items.generalization: Cell formatting operations may fail due to edge cases or version differences; isolate each style property modification in its own exception handler so successful copies still apply while failing ones are skipped.

memory_items.title: Verification Before Final Write
memory_items.lesson: Validate critical cell values match expectations after manipulation but before saving
memory_items.generalization: After any data transformation, verify key cells contain expected values (via print logging or assertions) before committing changes to disk, enabling early detection of logic errors.

memory_items.title: Dynamic Column Location Strategy
memory_items.lesson: Search for target columns by header content rather than assuming fixed positions
memory_items.generalization: Always locate relevant columns dynamically by iterating through header rows and matching content, making scripts resilient to column reordering across different file versions.

edits.content:
## Advanced Error Recovery Patterns

### Style Property Handling in openpyxl
- Use `hasattr()` or `getattr()` before accessing style attributes (fill, font, border, alignment)
- Always construct NEW style objects (`Font()`, `PatternFill()`, `Border()`, `Alignment()`) with explicit parameters
- Avoid direct assignment like `target_cell.font = source_cell.font` which causes TypeErrors
- Wrap each style property modification in individual `try/except` blocks

### Defensive Coding for Cell Manipulation
- Clear target cell values (`cell.value = None`) before populating new data
- Iterate through headers to find columns dynamically instead of hardcoding positions
- Verify critical cells after manipulation but before saving file
- Log row-by-row progress for easier debugging of batch operations

### Common openpyxl Pitfalls
- `fill.type` doesn't exist; use `fill.fill_type` or `getattr(fill, 'fill_type', None)`
- `PatternFill` expects `start_color`/`end_color` as string hex codes, not RGB objects
- `StyleProxy` objects cannot be assigned directly; recreate explicit style objects
- Border sides require `Side(style=...)` objects, not just style strings
